messages.content: You are a senior HVAC controls engineer extracting official ASHRAE Guideline 36 knowledge. Extract concise, high-value structured knowledge from the provided section only. Treat this as an industry standard/guideline source, not an OEM manual. Preserve section citations and exact evidence. Allowed knowledge_type values: operational_sequence, parameter_spec, fault_diagnostic_rule, commissioning_procedure, application_guidance. Prefer actionable control logic: enable/disable logic, staging rules, trim-and-respond reset rules, alarm/fault equations, setpoints/timers/ranges, and commissioning or TAB checks. Do not extract foreword/legal text, generic definitions, repeated table of contents lines, bibliography, marketing language, or ungrounded paraphrases. Return strict JSON with fields candidates and skipped_reason.

message.content:
{
  "candidates": [
    {
      "knowledge_type": "operational_sequence",
      "title": "Zone Group Definition and Mode Synchronization",
      "section_id": "5.4.1",
      "section_title": "5.4 Zone Groups",
      "equipment_scope": "Air handler and terminal units",
      "summary": "Each system is divided into Zone Groups (collections of zones served by a single air handler). All zones in a Zone Group must be in the same zone-group operating mode; if one zone enters a mode other than Unoccupied (due to override, sequence logic, or scheduled occupancy), all zones in that group enter that mode. Occupied-standby is a zonal subset of Occupied Mode and is not a zone-group mode.",
      "trigger_condition": "Any zone in a Zone Group is placed in a zone-group operating mode other than Unoccupied Mode.",
      "required_behavior": "All zones in that Zone Group shall enter that mode.",
      "configurable_values": [],
      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "All zones in each Zone Group shall be in the same zone-group operating mode as defined in Section 5.4.6. If one zone in a Zone Group is placed in any zone-group operating mode other than Unoccupied Mode (due to override, sequence logic, or scheduled occupancy), all zones in that Zone Group shall enter that mode.",
      "page_hint": 71,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "knowledge_type": "commissioning_procedure",
      "title": "Zone Group Testing/Commissioning Override Switches",
      "section_id": "5.4.5",
      "section_title": "5.4 Zone Groups",
      "equipment_scope": "Zone Group and terminal units",
      "summary": "For each Zone Group, provide a set of testing/commissioning software switches that override all zones in the group. When a Zone Group override switch is changed, the corresponding override switch for every zone in the group changes to the same value. Subsequently, zone-level switches may be changed independently; zone-group switch changes only affect zone-level switches when the group switch is changed.",
      "trigger_condition": "Commissioning agent changes a Zone Group override switch.",
      "required_behavior": "All zone-level override switches in that group shall change to the same value. Zone-level switches may then be changed independently without affecting the group switch.",
      "configurable_values": [],
      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": "Verify that changing a Zone Group override switch updates all zone-level switches accordingly, and that subsequent zone-level changes do not revert the group switch.",
      "evidence_quote": "When the value of a Zone Group’s override switch is changed, the corresponding override switch for every zone in the Zone Group shall change to the same value. Subsequently, the zone-level override switch may be changed to a different value.",
      "page_hint": 71,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "knowledge_type": "operational_sequence",
      "title": "Zone Group Occupied Mode Conditions",
      "section_id": "5.4.6.1",
      "section_title": "5.4.6 Zone-Group Operating Modes",
      "equipment_scope": "Zone Group",
      "summary": "A Zone Group is in Occupied Mode when either (a) the time is between the scheduled occupied start and stop times, or (b) the schedules have been overridden by the occupant override system.",
      "trigger_condition": "Time between scheduled occupied start and stop, or occupant override active.",
      "required_behavior": "Zone Group enters Occupied Mode.",
      "configurable_values": ["Scheduled occupied start time", "Scheduled occupied stop time"],
      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "A Zone Group is in the Occupied Mode when any of the following is true: a. The time of day is between the Zone Group’s scheduled occupied start and stop times. b. The schedules have been overridden by the occupant override system.",
      "page_hint": 71,
      "confidence": 0.95
    }
  ],
  "skipped_reason": null
}

message.content:
{
  "candidates": [
    {
      "knowledge_type": "operational_sequence",
      "title": "Cooling-Only VAV Zone Control Logic",
      "section_id": "5.5.5",
      "section_title": "5.5 VAV Terminal Unit—Cooling Only",
      "equipment_scope": "Cooling-only VAV terminal unit",
      "summary": "Active airflow setpoint is determined by zone state: cooling maps cooling loop output from minimum to cooling maximum; deadband sets to minimum; heating maps heating loop output from minimum to heating maximum. Damper modulates to maintain measured airflow at setpoint.",
      "trigger_condition": "Zone state changes (cooling, deadband, heating)",
      "required_behavior": "When cooling: map cooling-loop output to active airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature > room temperature, setpoint ≤ minimum endpoint. When deadband: setpoint = minimum endpoint. When heating: map heating-loop output to active airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature < room temperature + 3°C (5°F), setpoint ≤ minimum endpoint. Modulate damper to maintain measured airflow at setpoint.",
      "configurable_values": ["Vmin*", "Vcool-max", "Vheat-max"],
      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "When the Zone State is cooling, the cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.",
      "page_hint": 74,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "knowledge_type": "parameter_spec",
      "title": "Active Airflow Setpoint Endpoints by Zone Group Mode",
      "section_id": "5.5.4",
      "section_title": "5.5 VAV Terminal Unit—Cooling Only",
      "equipment_scope": "Cooling-only VAV terminal unit",
      "summary": "Endpoints for active airflow setpoint vary by zone group mode: Occupied uses Vcool-max for cooling max, Vmin* for min, Vheat-max for heating max; Cooldown and Setup use Vcool-max for cooling max, 0 for min and heating max; Warmup uses 0 for cooling max and min, Vcool-max for heating max; Setback uses 0 for cooling max and min, Vcool-max for heating max; Unoccupied uses 0 for all.",
      "trigger_condition": null,
      "required_behavior": null,
      "configurable_values": ["Vcool-max", "Vmin*", "Vheat-max"],
      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "Table 5.5.4 Endpoints as a Function of Zone Group Mode",
      "page_hint": 73,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "knowledge_type": "fault_diagnostic_rule",
      "title": "Low Airflow Alarm",
      "section_id": "5.5.6.1",
      "section_title": "5.5 VAV Terminal Unit—Cooling Only",
      "equipment_scope": "Cooling-only VAV terminal unit",
      "summary": "If measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate Level 4 alarm. If < 50% for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate Level 3 alarm. Suppress alarms for zones with importance multiplier 0 for static pressure reset T&R.",
      "trigger_condition": "Measured airflow < 70% or < 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0",
      "required_behavior": "Generate Level 4 alarm for <70%, Level 3 for <50%. Suppress if zone importance multiplier = 0.",
      "configurable_values": ["70% threshold", "50% threshold", "10 minutes", "importance multiplier"],
      "fault_condition": "Low airflow",
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.",
      "page_hint": 74,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "knowledge_type": "fault_diagnostic_rule",
      "title": "Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm",
      "section_id": "5.5.6.2",
      "section_title": "5.5 VAV Terminal Unit—Cooling Only",
      "equipment_scope": "Cooling-only VAV terminal unit",
      "summary": "If fan serving zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading > max(10% of Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm.",
      "trigger_condition": "Fan OFF and airflow reading > max(10% Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes",
      "required_behavior": "Generate Level 3 alarm",
      "configurable_values": ["10% of Vcool-max", "50 cfm", "30 minutes"],
      "fault_condition": "Airflow sensor calibration error",
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.",
      "page_hint": 74,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "knowledge_type": "fault_diagnostic_rule",
      "title": "Leaking Damper Alarm",
      "section_id": "5.5.6.3",
      "section_title": "5.5 VAV Terminal Unit—Cooling Only",
      "equipment_scope": "Cooling-only VAV terminal unit",
      "summary": "If damper position is 0% and airflow sensor reading > max(10% of Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 10 minutes while fan is proven on, generate Level 4 alarm.",
      "trigger_condition": "Damper 0% and airflow > max(10% Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 10 minutes while fan proven on",
      "required_behavior": "Generate Level 4 alarm",
      "configurable_values": ["10% of Vcool-max", "50 cfm", "10 minutes"],
      "fault_condition": "Leaking damper",
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.",
      "page_hint": 74,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "knowledge_type": "commissioning_procedure",
      "title": "Testing/Commissioning Overrides for Cooling-Only VAV",
      "section_id": "5.5.7",
      "section_title": "5.5 VAV Terminal Unit—Cooling Only",
      "equipment_scope": "Cooling-only VAV terminal unit",
      "summary": "Provide software switches interlocked to system-level point to force zone airflow setpoint to zero, Vcool-max, or Vmin; force damper full closed/open; and reset request-hours accumulator. All hardware points can be overridden through BAS at zone-group level.",
      "trigger_condition": null,
      "required_behavior": "Provide software switches for forcing setpoints and damper positions, and resetting accumulators. Overrides interlocked at zone-group level.",
      "configurable_values": ["Vcool-max", "Vmin"],
      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": "CxA can check for leaking dampers by forcing all VAV boxes closed and recording AHU airflow.",
      "evidence_quote": "Provide software switches that interlock to a system-level point to a. force zone airflow setpoint to zero, b. force zone airflow setpoint to Vcool-max, c. force zone airflow setpoint to Vmin, d. force damper full closed/open, and e. reset request-hours accumulator point to zero.",
      "page_hint": 74,
      "confidence": 0.95
    }
  ],
  "skipped_reason": null
}

      "knowledge_type": "operational_sequence",

      "section_id": "5.6.5",

      "equipment_scope": "VAV terminal unit with reheat coil",
      "summary": "When Zone State is cooling, map cooling-loop output to active airflow setpoint from cooling minimum to cooling maximum endpoint; heating coil disabled unless DAT below minimum setpoint. When deadband, active airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint; heating coil disabled unless DAT below minimum setpoint. When heating, from 0-50% heating-loop output resets DAT setpoint from AHU SAT to MaxΔT above space temp, airflow at heating minimum endpoint; from 51-100%, if DAT > room temp + 3°C (5°F), reset airflow from heating minimum to heating maximum endpoint. Heating coil modulated to maintain DAT setpoint.",
      "trigger_condition": "Zone State changes (cooling, deadband, heating)",
      "required_behavior": "Cooling: map loop output to airflow setpoint between cooling min and max endpoints. Deadband: set airflow to minimum endpoint. Heating: first 50% output resets DAT setpoint, airflow at heating min; above 50% if DAT > room+5°F, reset airflow to heating max. Modulate heating coil to maintain DAT setpoint.",
      "configurable_values": ["Vmin", "Vcool-max", "Vheat-min", "Vheat-max", "MaxΔT"],
      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "When the Zone State is cooling, the cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint from the cooling minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. Heating coil is disabled unless the DAT is below the minimum setpoint",

messages.content: You are a senior HVAC controls engineer extracting official ASHRAE Guideline 36 knowledge. Extract concise, high-value structured knowledge from the provided section only. Treat this as an industry standard/guideline source, not an OEM manual. Preserve section citations and exact evidence. Prefer actionable control logic: enable/disable logic, staging rules, trim-and-respond reset rules, alarm/fault equations, setpoints/timers/ranges, and commissioning or TAB checks. Do not extract foreword/legal text, generic definitions, repeated table of contents lines, bibliography, marketing language, or ungrounded paraphrases. For long sections, return the most operationally important items instead of one item per bullet. evidence_quote MUST be a SHORT verbatim contiguous substring copied from the section text, preferably the exact section heading line or one short requirement sentence. Do not use ellipses. Do not combine non-contiguous bullets into one quote. Keep evidence_quote under 35 words when possible. If you cannot quote it exactly, skip that item. Return strict JSON with fields candidates and skipped_reason.
